business.headline: Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Attractive or risky for long-term investors?Founded in 2016, OnEMI operates in the fast-growing digital lending space. It offers personal loans and loans against property with quick approvals and minimal paperwork—something that has clearly clicked with young borrowers.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex was down 1,215.91 points, or 1.54%, at 76,280.45 around 10:58 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 366.70 points, or 1.51%, to 23,811.25.

Infosys won't cut jobs despite AI shift, says CEO Salil ParekhInfosys CEO Salil Parekh said the company does not plan layoffs despite the AI shift. The stance sets it apart from peers and underscores a hiring-and-reskilling push.

Crude oil tops $120, highest since 2022: What it means for markets and rupeeDisruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route, have added to the uncertainty, taking crude oil prices to their highest levels since mid-2022.

8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. During these meetings, representatives from various employee groups are discussing key issues related to pay, allowances and service conditions.

Rajiv Bajaj to step down from Bajaj Finance board at end of termRajiv Bajaj's tenure as a non-executive director will conclude at the close of the company's upcoming annual general meeting, in line with regulatory requirements.

Odisha bank case explained: Rules for withdrawing money after deathOnce an account holder dies, the account is no longer treated as a normal account. Any access to funds becomes a claim settlement process, not a simple withdrawal.

Their exchange blended humour, new-parent life and the easy warmth fans rarely see online.

general news.headline: BJP to wrest Bengal from Trinamool with 192+ seats, predicts Today's ChanakyaThe BJP will wrest control of West Bengal from the ruling TMC after 15 years in a major political shift, predicts Today's Chanakya. According to the survey, the BJP is likely to get 192+ seats, while the TMC may secure 100+ seats.

Live: Today's Chanakya forecasts BJP sweep in Bengal with 48% vote shareWest Bengal Exit Poll Live Update: Uncertainty looms over the outcome of the West Bengal assembly elections 2026 as pollsters could not predict a one-way result, benefiting either the ruling Trinamool Congress or the opposition Bharatiya Janata Party. While the majority of the pollsters provided an edge to the BJP, two of them, People's Pulse and the Janmat Polls, said that the incumbent Trinamool Congress is returning with a thumping majority. In this context, suspense remains over the results. Several pollsters are expected to deliver their projections today. Catch all the live update at indiatoday.in.

There was no joint statement after the recent meeting in New Delhi that was described as "very tense". India as the BRICS president will have to do a tight rope walk ahead of the crucial summit in September.

Elite rail commandos seen securing Vande Bharat in J&KHeavily armed, black-clad commandos were spotted at Jammu Tawi railway station as the extended Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat Express was flagged off on Thursday. Live: Israel orders 15 Lebanese towns' residents to leave homes before strikesUS-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has rejected Iran's proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, choosing to tighten pressure instead of easing it. He argued the blockade is more effective than military strikes, signalling that Washington is relying on economic strain rather than immediate escalation to force Tehran's hand. That pressure is already showing. Iran's currency has slipped to a record low of 1,810,000 rials to the dollar, according to state-linked media, highlighting the growing toll on its economy. Behind the scenes, the US appears to be settling in for a longer standoff. Trump and his aides have held talks with energy executives on how to keep the blockade in place for months while trying to shield American consumers from rising costs. The political heat is rising at home as well. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the war during a congressional hearing, brushing aside criticism and calling opponents "the biggest adversary" as he faced lawmakers for the first time since the conflict began. Diplomacy, meanwhile, remains stuck. Pakistan is expected to receive a revised Iranian proposal, but Trump has shown little patience, warning Tehran to "better get smart soon." He also spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin, who offered to help address Iran's uranium stockpiles, adding another layer to the ongoing negotiations. At the same time, Israel has launched fresh strikes in southern Lebanon, killing emergency workers and soldiers. Israeli forces have made it clear there is "no ceasefire" in their campaign against Hezbollah, despite ongoing efforts for direct talks. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

India pulls off a power miracle. A big thanks to the SunIndia touched an all-time peak power demand without any shortage, and it was solar that stepped up at the most critical hour, supplying nearly 21% of the electricity. This moment underscores a quiet but decisive shift that the sun is no longer just a supplement but a key driver of India's energy resilience.

Iran to secure Gulf region, eliminate enemy's abuses, Mojtaba warns USIn a rare message, Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who has remained hidden from public view, warned that 'aliens' creating mischief in the Persian Gulf would be sent to the 'depths of its waters'.

Rain, hail hits Delhi-NCR: Orange alert issued, weather system to worsenThe dual impact of cooling rains and gusty winds is expected to bring daytime temperatures down noticeably in affected areas, offering a temporary break before conditions potentially stabilise again.

No need to humiliate me with arrest, Pawan Khera tells SC at bail hearingThe Supreme Court heard Pawan Khera's anticipatory bail plea in a defamation case, with his lawyer Abhishek Manu Singhvi accusing Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma of bias and calling his remarks "constitutional cowboy."

Deceived, used: US journalist as Pak event turns into govt-bashing showAn informal media gathering in Pakistan involving foreign reporters, who were stationed for the US-Iran talks, turned into a full-blown controversy. What was supposed to be a meet-and-greet event turned into a political spectacle after two Pakistani journalists used the platform to criticise the government and the military.

Bali streets piled with trash after island's largest landfill declared off-limitsBali is facing a mounting waste crisis after authorities restricted organic waste at its largest landfill without ready alternatives, leaving garbage piling up in streets, attracting pests and forcing residents to burn it.

1 litre of ethanol needs 10,000 litres water: Hidden cost of India's fuel pushIndia's total ethanol production capacity stands at 1,822 crore litres, with a disproportionate share concentrated in already water-stressed states like Delhi and Maharashtra.

How hunger damages the body, brain and immune systemAccording to the Global Report on Food Crises, around 35.5 million children were acutely malnourished in the 23 countries/territories that were experiencing a nutrition crisis in 2025.

sports.headline: MCC defends umpires in Angkrish Raghuvanshi Obstructing the Field dismissal call The MCC defended the umpire's decision to give Angkrish Raghuvanshi out obstructing the field against Lucknow Super Giants, stating it aligns with long-standing laws that penalise wilful changes of running direction.

Indonesia's Thomas Cup exit, a historic first in 68 years Indonesia's shock Thomas Cup group-stage exit ends a 68-year run of dominance, exposing a rare crack in badminton's most enduring empire and possibly marking a defining shift in the sport's power balance.
